uk: Неділя перед Різдвом Христовим, Святих Отців. Глас 5. Єв. 8.
🕃 Передсвяття Різдва Господа Бога і Спаса нашого Ісуса Христа
saints.en: Sunday before the Nativity, of the Holy Fathers. Forefeast of the Nativity of our Lord, God and Savior Jesus Christ. Hieromartyr Ignatius the God-Bearer

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» 10 стихир: 4 воскресні, 3 передсв. і 3 Отців, Сл.: Отців, І нині: передсвяття. Вхід. Прокімен дня і три читання Отців: Бут. 14, 14-20; Втор. 1, 8-17; Іс. 51, 4-8. Стиховня воскресна, Сл.: Отців, І нині: передсвяття. По «Отче наш»: тропар воскрес., Сл.: Отців, І нині: передсв. Відпуст великий з помин. неділі і свщмч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь» тропар воскрес. (2), Сл.: Отців, І нині: передсв. Катизми чергові, сідал. воскрес, «Ангельський собор» і сідальні Отців. Степенна і прокімен 5-го гласу. Єв. – Ів. зач. 64; 20, 11-18. Пс. 50. Канон воскресний, передсвяття і Отців. Катавасія: «Христос народжується». По 3-ій – іпакой Отців. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос Отців. По 9-ій – «Свят Господь Бог наш» (3) і світильний воскрес., Сл.: Отців, І нині: передсв. На Хвалитних – 8 стихир: 4 воскресних і 4 Отців з двома їхніми приспівами, Сл.: Отців, І нині: «Преблагословенна». Після Великого славосл. – тропар «Сьогодні спасіння світу». Відпуст великий з помин. Отців і свщмч. Після відпусту Сл., І нині: стихира євангельська.
Часи: на всіх – тропар воскрес., Сл.: Отців. Кондаки: на 1 і 6 – Отців, на 3 і 9 - передсв.
Літургія: антифони воскресні. Тропар воскрес., Отців і передсв., Сл.: кондак Отців, І нині: кондак передсв. Прокімен, апостол, алилуя і Євангеліє лише Отців. Ап. – Євр. 328 зач.; 11, 9-10. 17-23. 32-40. Єв. – Мт. 1 зач.; 1, 1-25. Причасний «Хваліте» і Отців.

troparion_en.text: Great are the accomplishments of faith,  / for the Three Holy Youths rejoiced in the source of the flame, as if by restful waters,1 /  and the Prophet Daniel appeared  / as a shepherd of lions as of sheep.  / By their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion_en.text: (For when the Sunday before the Nativity falls on December 20-24)Be glad, O Bethlehem! Prepare yourself, O Ephratha;  /  for the Lamb is on her way to give birth to the Great Shepherd she carries in her womb.  /  The God-bearing Fathers will rejoice, beholding Him,  /  and with the shepherds, they will praise the Virgin who nurses Him.

troparion.text: Great are the accomplishments of faith,  / for the Three Holy Youths rejoiced in the source of the flame, as if by restful waters,1 /  and the Prophet Daniel appeared  / as a shepherd of lions as of sheep.  / By their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion.text: (For when the Sunday before the Nativity falls on December 20-24)Be glad, O Bethlehem! Prepare yourself, O Ephratha;  /  for the Lamb is on her way to give birth to the Great Shepherd she carries in her womb.  /  The God-bearing Fathers will rejoice, beholding Him,  /  and with the shepherds, they will praise the Virgin who nurses Him.

oca_saints.name: Forefeast of the Nativity of our Lord

troparion.text: Prepare, O Bethlehem, for Eden has been opened to all! /  Adorn yourself, O Ephratha, for the Tree of Life blossoms forth from the Virgin in the cave! /  Her womb is a spiritual paradise planted with the Divine Fruit; / if we eat of it, we shall live forever and not die like Adam. /  Christ is born to raise up again what fell in former times, His image.

kontakion.text: Today the Virgin comes to the cave /  to give birth ineffably to the pre-eternal Word. /  Hearing this, rejoice, O inhabited earth!  /  With the angels and the shepherds glorify /  the pre-eternal God, Whose will it was to appear as a young child!

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Ignatius the God-Bearer, Bishop of Antioch

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Ignatius, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

kontakion.text: The stirring celebration of your victorious fight / is an announcement of the One who is to be born of the Virgin. / In your eagerness to possess Him forever, / you hastened to be devoured by the wild beasts. / Therefore, O glorious Ignatius, you were called the bearer of God!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Ignatius, Archimandrite of the Kiev Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Ignatius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Philogonius, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: Saint Daniel II, Archbishop of Serbia

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Daniel, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: Repose of Saint John of Kronstadt

troparion.text: With the apostles your message has gone out to the ends of the world, / and with the confessors you suffered for Christ! / You are like the hierarchs through your preaching of the word; / with the righteous you are radiant with the grace of God. / The Lord has exalted your humility above the heavens / and given us your name as a source of miracles. / O wonder-worker, living in Christ for ever, / have mercy on those beset by troubles; / and hear us when we cry out in faith, O our beloved shepherd John!

kontakion.text: This day the pastor of Kronstadt / appears before the throne of God / praying fervently on behalf of the faithful / to the chief pastor Christ, who has promised: / “I will build my church, and the powers of death shall not prevail against it!”

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God the “Rescuer of the Drowning”

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Thou Who wast raised up...”)
Let us, who are sinful and humble, now flee fervently to the Theotokos,  / bowing down to her, crying out in repentance from the depths of our soul:  / Hasten, O Sovereign Lady, help us and take pity on us,  / for we are perishing from a multitude of transgressions.  / Do not turn your servants away empty-handed, for we have you as our only hope.

kontakion.text: Steadfast Protectress of Christians,  / constant advocate before the Creator,  / do not despise the entreaties of us sinners,  / but in your goodness hasten to help us who cry out to you with faith: /  Speedily hear our petition and intercede for us, O Theotokos,  / for you always protect those who honor you.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr John, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr John;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.
